mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 55% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises below 0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and builder demand, causing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization and margin expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 0.3% monthly growth, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 in low-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.03, or the relationship reverses during periods of labor market saturation.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 5.0 percent triggers financial stress and higher borrowing costs that compress equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures signals inventory accumulation and weakening consumer demand, causing negative equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth > 2.0% while RSAFS declines month-over-month
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) or Rank IC falls below 0.02 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of high unemployment indicates a severe supply-side contraction that reduces corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in over 50% of triggered instances, or the mechanism loses predictive power when unemployment falls below 5.0%.
